design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through comic book panels by defeating enemies or solving puzzles, using collected items and physical interactions with the environment
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Health depletion from combat and environmental hazards; limited continues system creates resource scarcity pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Being trapped inside a living comic book world; breaking the fourth wall by physically manipulating comic panels (tearing pages, ripping items from panels)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game commits entirely to a comic book aesthetic and mechanical metaphor—players interact with the UI itself as game objects (paper planes, page manipulation). The narrative conceit (comic artist transported into his creation) is fully integrated into gameplay rather than separated as story wrapper. Refuses easy accessibility in favor of challenging combat execution.

difficulty.lives_system: Single health bar depletion ends game; fall damage also causes game over
difficulty.continues_system: Continue available upon completing each episode; limited continue supply creates permanent consequence for failure
difficulty.checkpointing: Episode-based checkpointing; death returns to last episode start, not mid-level
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal—no selectable difficulty modes documented; puzzle branching paths offer some exploration choice but combat difficulty is fixed

progression.upgrade_system: Item inventory system (max 3 items); items provide tactical advantages in combat and puzzle-solving; iced tea is sole healing mechanic
progression.unlock_mechanism: Six levels across three episodes; progression is linear through panel sequence with occasional branching panel choices that converge back to single path
replayability_hooks: Branching panel paths offer exploration variety on replays
Item discovery via Roadkill rat mechanic encourages thorough level searching

world_building_techniques: Speech balloons integrated into comic panel environment
Hand-drawn American superhero comic aesthetic for all visuals
Post-apocalyptic world setting (decimated NYC, Himalayas, Zealand atolls) across six themed episodes
Character-driven narrative: Sketch Turner (creator transported into creation) paired with Alissa Cyan (resistance fighter)

notable_bosses: Mortus—primary antagonist, mutant character who escaped from comic pages; climactic fight near end of game

faction_indicators: Alissa Cyan's defense force/resistance against Mortus regime
danger_signals: Nuclear weapon subplot (weapons factory, derelict ship)
Mortus seeking physical form in real world (reality breach threat)
environmental_storytelling: Decimated NYC environments show urban destruction
Comic panel structure itself is a narrative device—progression is literal movement through story pages
Panel branching choices reflect player agency within authored story

sources_used: Wikipedia - Comix Zone (gameplay, synopsis, development, difficulty reception)
